text.title: Market Research Tool Reference
text.summary: CLI/SaaS reference table, pipeline prompt template, and agent gotchas for multi-stage market research pipelines.

list.item: pytrends — Google Trends trend signal (pip install pytrends)
serpapi — Structured search results (pip install google-search-results)
anthropic SDK — Orchestration and synthesis (pip install anthropic)
httpx — Async API calls to Perplexity, Contify (pip install httpx)
pandas — Tabulate and merge multi-tool outputs (pip install pandas)
Perplexity Pro — REST API, exploration and cited synthesis
ChatGPT API — REST API, general synthesis; weaker citations than Perplexity
Claude API — REST API, best for structured output and multi-step reasoning
Crayon — partial webhooks, competitor intel; manual setup required
Klue — partial, competitor intel; better NLP querying than Crayon
Contify — partial REST, competitor + market news
Brandwatch — partial REST, social sentiment; complex setup
Statista AI — export only; market data reports
CB Insights — partial export; company and funding data
Exploding Topics — REST API; emerging trend signals
Dovetail — partial export; interview and UX research synthesis

section.title: Prompt Pattern

example.code: <research_pipeline> <topic>AI research tools market, 2026</topic> <stages> <stage name="exploration" tool="perplexity">Broad landscape: who are the players, what are the use cases?</stage> <stage name="competitor-intel" tool="serpapi">Recent news, funding, product launches for top 5 players</stage> <stage name="trend" tool="google-trends">Search volume trends for key terms over 12 months</stage> <stage name="synthesis" tool="claude-sonnet">Merge stage outputs into a structured report with source registry</stage> </stages> <output>Markdown report, source registry, coverage gaps list</output> </research_pipeline>
example.note: Each stage declares its tool explicitly. Synthesis is a separate stage, not embedded in any tool call.

section.reference: Orchestrator that selects tool per stage and generates stage queries
XML prompt for multi-stage market research pipeline

antipattern.description: "AI tool" in 2026 means both AI-native tools (Perplexity, Elicit) and legacy tools with AI features bolted on (Statista AI, SurveyMonkey AI); their reliability for agent pipelines differs significantly.
antipattern.reason: Always include the API reference in the agent's context for each tool. Agents will hallucinate capabilities for unfamiliar tools if given only the tool name.

antipattern.description: Tool outputs have different schemas; the synthesis step receives inconsistent data unless normalized.
antipattern.reason: Perplexity → citations as URLs; SerpAPI → structured JSON; Google Trends → DataFrames. The orchestrator must normalize before the synthesis call, not after.
